root_text.sn35.7:1.3: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ cakkhusmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.7:1.5: paccuppannassa cakkhussa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn35.7:1.10: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītāya jivhāya anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.7:1.12: paccuppannāya jivhāya nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn35.7:1.16: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ manasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.7:1.18: paccuppannassa manassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.7:0.3: The Interior as Impermanent in the Three Times
translation_text.sn35.7:1.1: “Mendicants, the eye of the past and future is impermanent,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.3: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with the eye of the past,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.4: they don’t look forward to enjoying the eye in the future,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.5: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding the eye in the present.
translation_text.sn35.7:1.14: mind of the past and future is impermanent,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.16: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple doesn’t worry about the mind of the past,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.17: they don’t look forward to enjoying the mind in the future,
translation_text.sn35.7:1.18: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding the mind in the present.”

reference_text.sn35.7:1.1: bj16.10, cck18.5, dr18.4, ms13S4_15, msdiv7, pts-vp-pli4.4, vri26.4
